pattern: The three pairwise-distance limits only ever depend on the ordered triple itself, so a direct triple scan with an early bound check on the first pair is already fast enough at this scale.
hints: The three conditions are independent pairwise distance checks on an ordered triple of positions, so a direct nested scan over i < j < k already fits comfortably within the size limits here.
Check the i-j distance as soon as j is picked so you can skip the innermost loop entirely when that pair already fails.
Only enter the innermost loop over k when the first bound holds, then verify the remaining two distance conditions there.

edge_inputs.description: tight bounds with several repeated values
minimum length array of three elements
all bounds zero require exact equality throughout
very large bounds count every triplet
one asymmetric bound fails while the others pass
values spread across the full allowed range
zero bounds with no repeated values give zero triplets

stress.description: moderate array near the maximum length with random bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    arr = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    a = rng.randint(0, 1000)
    b = rng.randint(0, 1000)
    c = rng.randint(0, 1000)
    return [arr, a, b, c]
